content:
# Per-Keyframe Adaptive Depth Zones — Design Decision (2026-04-04)

## What
Replaces global depth zone computation (single `adaptive_zone_boundaries` call per clip) with a two-tier architecture:

**Tier 1 — Base LUT (32 fine zones, per-segment):** Built from all keyframes within a scene segment. 32 zones give fine depth resolution across [0.0–1.0]. Many keyframes contribute → good 33³ LUT cell coverage. Rebuilt only at scene segment boundaries. Encodes color science.

**Tier 2 — Runtime Zones (8 adaptive zones, per-keyframe):** Computed from each keyframe's own depth map. Places zone centers where depth values actually ARE in each frame. Combined GPU texture rebuilt per-keyframe at local zone centers, reading from 32-zone base LUT. Encodes depth context.

## Why
Global zones average depth distributions across disparate scenes (reef close-up vs open water). This causes: (1) LUT calibration trained on wrong depth partitions, (2) per-frame grading applied at wrong depth sample points. Both problems compound when camera moves significantly or subjects shift depth.

## Key Architecture
- Scene segments detected via Wasserstein-1 distance on depth histograms between consecutive keyframes
- All per-KF zone boundaries pre-computed after Pass 0 (all depth maps known upfront), smoothed over 3-KF window
- Per-segment base LUTs + HSL corrections built in pre-compute phase (~500ms total)
- Pipelined double-buffer GPU texture build: Stream B builds next KF's combined texture while Stream A grades current frames — zero visible stall
- Dual-texture temporal blending in grading kernel: non-KF frames sample both adjacent KF textures, lerp by temporal position
- VRAM: ~246MB total for grading (32-zone base LUT + 2 × combined texture sets), fits easily in 6GB after models unloaded

## Performance Impact
- Pre-compute: +500ms one-time
- Per non-KF frame: ~50-70% slower (dual texture sampling)
- Per KF frame (t=0): unchanged (skip second texture)
- Texture rebuilds: hidden behind grading work via CUDA stream pipelining

## Spec
`docs/decisions/2026-04-04-per-keyframe-adaptive-depth-zones.md`
